project_context.plan:
Language: C++ Things to be implemented
1. TLB cache 2. Page table (One per process) 3. Physical Memory Manager => responsible for handling frames Will maintain free frame list 4. Page Replacement Policy => responsible for eviction logic (LRU stack, FIFO, etc.) Separating 3 and 4 follows SRP and makes debugging page faults much easier.
Now, TLB will have the following struct for each entry => [ tag (page num) | frame num | access control bits | valid bit | dirty bit | asid ]
The page table will have the following struct for each entry => [ frame number | valid bit | dirty bit | access control | swap space offset ]
The main MMU unit (class) will maintain: 1. TLB miss rate 2. Page fault rate 3. PTBR (Page Table Base Register) -> Points to the active process's page table.
It will also provide 2 global methods: 1. convert_address(logical_addr, access_type) 2. get_stats(stats& stat) => populates the stat struct
It will also have a protected method for allocating a frame.
Address conversion takes the following steps: 1. Extraction of page number and offset. 2. Check PTBR to ensure we are translating for the correct active process. 3. Using page number: 1. Check TLB for a match (using page num as tag) AND ensure ASID matches current process: a. if TLB hit AND valid bit is 1: 1. Check if access level requested is permitted. If not, throw Access Denied (Segfault). 2. If access_type == WRITE, set dirty bit = 1 in TLB. 3. Get frame num, merge with offset, return physical address.
b. if TLB miss (or valid bit is 0): 1. Access the Page Table (via PTBR) using page num as index. 2. Check the VALID bit. if valid == 0 (PAGE FAULT): request frame allocation from Physical Memory Manager. update page fault rate. load page from swap space offset into the newly allocated frame. update page table entry (frame = new frame, valid = 1, dirty = 0).
3. Check if access level requested is permitted. If not, throw Access Denied. 4. If access_type == WRITE, set dirty bit = 1 in Page Table.
5. Save page table entry into TLB (evicting an old TLB entry if necessary). 6. Get frame number, construct physical address, return.
For page allocation in case of page fault: 1. Look at free frame list. 2. If not empty: pop frame, return frame number.
3. If empty (need eviction): consult Page Replacement Policy (e.g., LRU) to pick a victim frame. use a reverse-mapping table to find WHICH page table entry currently maps to this victim frame. check the dirty bit of that page table entry. if dirty == 1, write the frame's contents to the swap space offset.
set the valid bit of that page table entry to 0 (invalidating it for the old process). return the newly freed frame number.
project_context.folder_structure: virtual_memory_sim/ ├── CMakeLists.txt (or Makefile) ├── src/ │ ├── main.cpp # Entry point, file I/O (reading addresses.txt) │ ├── core/ │ │ ├── MMU.h / .cpp # The main Memory Management Unit │ │ └── System.h / .cpp # Manages processes and coordinates the simulation │ ├── memory/ │ │ ├── PhysicalMemory.h / .cpp # Manages the byte array and free frames │ │ └── BackingStore.h / .cpp # Handles file I/O for BACKING_STORE.bin │ ├── paging/ │ │ ├── PageTable.h / .cpp # Per-process page table logic │ │ ├── TLB.h / .cpp # Translation Lookaside Buffer cache │ │ └── Types.h # Shared structs (TLBEntry, PageTableEntry, Stats) │ └── policy/ │ ├── IReplacement.h # Interface for replacement policies │ ├── LRUPolicy.h / .cpp # LRU Implementation │ └── FIFOPolicy.h / .cpp # FIFO Implementation └── data/ ├── addresses.txt ├── correct.txt └── BACKING_STORE.bin
